1	# Ralph v2 Progress Log
2	Started: Mon 13 Apr 2026 05:04:13 AM EDT
3	PRD: Transform the SIGNAL terminal into a competitive financial intelligence platform: sync AI backfill to production, fix ticker recommendation pipeline, add dynamic column customization, integrate Polygon.io real-time market data, and build comprehensive company profiles with SEC EDGAR institutional/insider data.
4	Mode: Adversarial (loop)
5	---
6	
7	## Codebase Patterns
8	
9	- **mypy config**: `pyproject.toml` has `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` — needed because many deps lack stubs (boto3, firebase_admin, etc.)
10	- **database.py kwargs**: Use `dict[str, Any]` for engine kwargs to avoid mypy narrowing errors when mixing bool/int/type values
11	- **Script conventions**: Scripts in `scripts/` use `sys.path.insert(0, ...)` + `dotenv` load + `init_db(Config.DATABASE_URL)` + `get_session()` pattern
12	- **News.link is unique**: The `link` column is the unique identifier for articles across local and production DBs
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 2026-04-13 09:05 EDT - US-001 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented export_ai_backfill.py
17	
18	- Created `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py` — exports AI fields as SQL UPDATE statements keyed on `link`
19	- Supports `--dry-run` (count + preview) and `--output <path>` (full SQL dump)
20	- SQL wraps in BEGIN/COMMIT, handles NULLs, booleans, float formatting, single-quote escaping
21	- Fixed `app/database.py` kwargs typing (dict[str, Any]) for mypy compatibility
22	- Added `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` to `pyproject.toml`
23	- Verified: 31,067 records exported, mypy clean, all PRD test assertions pass
24	- **Files changed:** `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py`, `app/database.py`, `pyproject.toml`
25	- **Environment notes for Tester:**
26	  - Local SQLite DB has 31,067 AI-analyzed articles — `--dry-run` will show this count
27	  - `--output` requires a writable path (e.g., `/tmp/ai_backfill.sql`)
28	  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` (not in requirements.txt by default)
29	  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` (stale tier config values) — not related to this story
30	---
31

33	
34	- **All 5 test assertions passed**, all 5 acceptance criteria met
35	- Executed `--dry-run`: exits 0, prints "Found 31067 AI-analyzed articles to export" with 10-line preview
36	- Executed `--output`: exits 0, created 40MB SQL file (37744 lines, 31067 UPDATE statements)
37	- Verified SQL structure: BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping, all 9 AI fields in SET clauses, WHERE keyed on `link`
38	- Verified single-quote escaping (`''`), boolean formatting (TRUE/FALSE), float decimals
39	- mypy: "Success: no issues found in 1 source file"

42	- **Artifacts:** `ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/` (dryrun_stdout.log, export_stdout.log, mypy_stdout.log, pytest_stdout.log, output/ai_backfill.sql)
43	---
44	
45	## 2026-04-13 09:17 EDT - US-002 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented import_ai_backfill.py
46	
47	- Created `scripts/import_ai_backfill.py` — imports AI backfill SQL dump into target DATABASE_URL
48	- Parses UPDATE statements via regex, converts SQL literals back to Python values
49	- Uses parameterized SQLAlchemy queries for execution (safe from SQL injection)
50	- Runs in a single transaction with full rollback on error
51	- Queries target DB to identify articles already having `ai_analyzed=True` — skips them
52	- `--dry-run` validates SQL and reports matched/skipped/unmatched/error counts without executing
53	- Handles multi-line reasoning fields and escaped single quotes in SQL literals

81	  - `BEDROCK_ENABLED` was read in `bedrock_config.py` but not referenced in `worker.py` or `config.py`
82	  - ECS task definition was missing `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID` in `shared_env`
83	  - Logging in `_run_bedrock_analysis` lacked start log, elapsed time, and per-article failure counts
84	- **Changes made:**
85	  - `app/config.py`: Added `BEDROCK_ENABLED` to `Config` class
86	  - `app/worker.py`: Logs `BEDROCK_ENABLED` status at worker startup
87	  - `infra/stack.py`: Added `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID: "MiniMax-M2.7"` to `shared_env`
88	  - `app/services/feed_refresh.py`: Enhanced `_run_bedrock_analysis()` with structured logging — start log with batch_size, elapsed_seconds on all exit paths, success_count/failure_count tracking, skip log when disabled
89	- **Self-validation:** import ok, all 5 test assertions pass, mypy clean (2 files), pytest 86 passed (8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py)
90	- **Files changed:** `app/services/feed_refresh.py`, `app/worker.py`, `app/config.py`, `infra/stack.py`
91	- **Environment notes for Tester:**
92	  - Test assertions 1-3 are grep/import checks — run them directly
93	  - Test assertion 4: `python -m pytest tests/ -v -k 'feed or worker'` — 12 tests, all should pass
94	  - Test assertion 5: `mypy app/services/feed_refresh.py app/worker.py` — should report "Success: no issues found in 2 source files"
95	  - AC5 (prod verification) requires deploy: `cdk deploy` + `force-new-deployment` on worker service
96	  - After deploy, trigger `POST /api/refresh` on production and query for `ai_analyzed=True` on newly fetched articles
97	  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` are NOT related to this story
98	  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` if not already installed

# Ralph v2 Progress Log
Started: Mon 13 Apr 2026 05:04:13 AM EDT
PRD: Transform the SIGNAL terminal into a competitive financial intelligence platform: sync AI backfill to production, fix ticker recommendation pipeline, add dynamic column customization, integrate Polygon.io real-time market data, and build comprehensive company profiles with SEC EDGAR institutional/insider data.
Mode: Adversarial (loop)
---

## Codebase Patterns

- **mypy config**: `pyproject.toml` has `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` — needed because many deps lack stubs (boto3, firebase_admin, etc.)
- **database.py kwargs**: Use `dict[str, Any]` for engine kwargs to avoid mypy narrowing errors when mixing bool/int/type values
- **Script conventions**: Scripts in `scripts/` use `sys.path.insert(0, ...)` + `dotenv` load + `init_db(Config.DATABASE_URL)` + `get_session()` pattern
- **News.link is unique**: The `link` column is the unique identifier for articles across local and production DBs

---

## 2026-04-13 09:05 EDT - US-001 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented export_ai_backfill.py

- Created `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py` — exports AI fields as SQL UPDATE statements keyed on `link`
- Supports `--dry-run` (count + preview) and `--output <path>` (full SQL dump)
- SQL wraps in BEGIN/COMMIT, handles NULLs, booleans, float formatting, single-quote escaping
- Fixed `app/database.py` kwargs typing (dict[str, Any]) for mypy compatibility
- Added `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` to `pyproject.toml`
- Verified: 31,067 records exported, mypy clean, all PRD test assertions pass
- **Files changed:** `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py`, `app/database.py`, `pyproject.toml`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Local SQLite DB has 31,067 AI-analyzed articles — `--dry-run` will show this count
  - `--output` requires a writable path (e.g., `/tmp/ai_backfill.sql`)
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` (not in requirements.txt by default)
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` (stale tier config values) — not related to this story
---

## 2026-04-13 09:12 EDT - US-001 - TESTER — TEST_PASSED

- **All 5 test assertions passed**, all 5 acceptance criteria met
- Executed `--dry-run`: exits 0, prints "Found 31067 AI-analyzed articles to export" with 10-line preview
- Executed `--output`: exits 0, created 40MB SQL file (37744 lines, 31067 UPDATE statements)
- Verified SQL structure: BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping, all 9 AI fields in SET clauses, WHERE keyed on `link`
- Verified single-quote escaping (`''`), boolean formatting (TRUE/FALSE), float decimals
- mypy: "Success: no issues found in 1 source file"
- pytest: 86 passed, 8 failed (all 8 pre-existing in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py — stale tier config values)
- Source DB has 0 NULL AI fields across 31067 records — NULL code path not exercised but SQL format is valid
- **Artifacts:** `ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/` (dryrun_stdout.log, export_stdout.log, mypy_stdout.log, pytest_stdout.log, output/ai_backfill.sql)
---

## 2026-04-13 09:17 EDT - US-002 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented import_ai_backfill.py

- Created `scripts/import_ai_backfill.py` — imports AI backfill SQL dump into target DATABASE_URL
- Parses UPDATE statements via regex, converts SQL literals back to Python values
- Uses parameterized SQLAlchemy queries for execution (safe from SQL injection)
- Runs in a single transaction with full rollback on error
- Queries target DB to identify articles already having `ai_analyzed=True` — skips them
- `--dry-run` validates SQL and reports matched/skipped/unmatched/error counts without executing
- Handles multi-line reasoning fields and escaped single quotes in SQL literals
- Self-validated: 31,067 statements parsed (0 parse errors), all matched in local DB, all correctly skipped (already analyzed), mypy clean
- **Files changed:** `scripts/import_ai_backfill.py`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Tester must first generate a SQL dump: `python scripts/export_ai_backfill.py --output /tmp/ai_backfill.sql`
  - Then run: `python scripts/import_ai_backfill.py --dry-run --input /tmp/ai_backfill.sql`
  - When testing against local DB, expect: all 31,067 matched, all 31,067 skipped (already analyzed), 0 would update — this is correct because the export came from the same DB
  - Output includes keywords: `matched`, `skipped`, `errors` (test assertion #2)
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` (not in requirements.txt by default)
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` — not related to this story
---

## 2026-04-13 09:20 EDT - US-002 - TESTER — TEST_PASSED

- **All 3 test assertions passed**, all 6 acceptance criteria met
- Generated SQL dump prerequisite via `export_ai_backfill.py --output` (31067 records)
- Executed `--dry-run`: exits 0, parsed 31067 UPDATE statements (0 parse errors), printed summary with matched/skipped/unmatched/errors counts
- Executed real import (non-dry-run): exits 0, committed transaction, updated=0, skipped=31067 (all already had ai_analyzed=True) — confirms skip logic works correctly
- Tested malformed SQL input: parser correctly ignores invalid lines, reports unmatched articles for nonexistent links
- mypy: "Success: no issues found in 1 source file"
- pytest: 86 passed, 8 failed (all 8 pre-existing in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py — stale tier config values)
- **Artifacts:** `ralph/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/` (dryrun_stdout.log, real_import_stdout.log, malformed_dryrun.log, mypy_stdout.log, pytest_stdout.log, ai_backfill.sql, malformed_input.sql)
---

## 2026-04-13 09:25 EDT - US-003 - IMPLEMENTOR — Diagnosed and fixed production AI worker pipeline

- **Audit findings:**
  - `_run_bedrock_analysis()` already correctly called in `refresh_feeds_parallel()` after store + dedup (line 128-129)
  - `BEDROCK_ENABLED` was read in `bedrock_config.py` but not referenced in `worker.py` or `config.py`
  - ECS task definition was missing `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID` in `shared_env`
  - Logging in `_run_bedrock_analysis` lacked start log, elapsed time, and per-article failure counts
- **Changes made:**
  - `app/config.py`: Added `BEDROCK_ENABLED` to `Config` class
  - `app/worker.py`: Logs `BEDROCK_ENABLED` status at worker startup
  - `infra/stack.py`: Added `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID: "MiniMax-M2.7"` to `shared_env`
  - `app/services/feed_refresh.py`: Enhanced `_run_bedrock_analysis()` with structured logging — start log with batch_size, elapsed_seconds on all exit paths, success_count/failure_count tracking, skip log when disabled
- **Self-validation:** import ok, all 5 test assertions pass, mypy clean (2 files), pytest 86 passed (8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py)
- **Files changed:** `app/services/feed_refresh.py`, `app/worker.py`, `app/config.py`, `infra/stack.py`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Test assertions 1-3 are grep/import checks — run them directly
  - Test assertion 4: `python -m pytest tests/ -v -k 'feed or worker'` — 12 tests, all should pass
  - Test assertion 5: `mypy app/services/feed_refresh.py app/worker.py` — should report "Success: no issues found in 2 source files"
  - AC5 (prod verification) requires deploy: `cdk deploy` + `force-new-deployment` on worker service
  - After deploy, trigger `POST /api/refresh` on production and query for `ai_analyzed=True` on newly fetched articles
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` are NOT related to this story
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` if not already installed
---

content.content:
1	"""SEC EDGAR client — institutional holdings (13F), position changes (13D/13G), insider trades (Form 4).
2	
3	Provides EdgarClient with three main methods:
4	- get_institutional_holders(symbol): top institutional holders from 13F filings
5	- get_major_position_changes(symbol): recent 13D/13G filings
6	- get_insider_transactions(symbol): recent Form 4 insider transactions
7	
8	User-Agent set per SEC EDGAR policy. Rate limit: 10 req/sec via throttle.
9	No API key required — SEC EDGAR is free public data.
10	"""
11	
12	import logging
13	import time
14	from typing import Any, Optional
15	from xml.etree import ElementTree
16	
17	import requests
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger("signal.edgar")
20	
21	EDGAR_BASE_URL = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST"
22	EDGAR_DATA_URL = "https://data.sec.gov"
23	EDGAR_USER_AGENT = "InstantNews dev@instnews.net"
24	
25	# Cache TTLs per acceptance criteria
26	INSTITUTIONAL_TTL = 86400  # 24 hours for 13F
27	POSITION_CHANGE_TTL = 21600  # 6 hours for 13D/13G
28	INSIDER_TTL = 3600  # 1 hour for Form 4
29	
30	# SEC rate limit: 10 req/sec — minimum 0.1s between requests
31	_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL = 0.1
32	
33	
34	class _CacheEntry:
35	    __slots__ = ("value", "expires_at")
36	
37	    def __init__(self, value: Any, ttl: float) -> None:
38	        self.value = value
39	        self.expires_at = time.monotonic() + ttl
40	
41	    def is_valid(self) -> bool:
42	        return time.monotonic() < self.expires_at
43	
44	
45	class EdgarClient:
46	    """Client for SEC EDGAR filings with in-memory caching and rate limiting."""
47	
48	    def __init__(self) -> None:
49	        self._session = requests.Session()
50	        self._session.headers.update({
51	            "User-Agent": EDGAR_USER_AGENT,
52	            "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
53	        })
54	        self._last_request_time = 0.0
55	        self._cik_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
56	        self._institutional_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
57	        self._position_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
58	        self._insider_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
59	
60	    @property
61	    def enabled(self) -> bool:
62	        return True  # EDGAR is free, always enabled
63	
64	    def _throttle(self) -> None:
65	        """Enforce SEC EDGAR 10 req/sec rate limit."""
66	        now = time.monotonic()
67	        elapsed = now - self._last_request_time
68	        if elapsed < _MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL:
69	            time.sleep(_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL - elapsed)
70	        self._last_request_time = time.monotonic()
71	
72	    def _get(self, url: str, params: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,

245	    def _parse_13dg_filing(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> tuple[str, Optional[float], Optional[int]]:

247	        filer_name = "Unknown Filer"

252	            # Fetch the filing index to get filer info

256	            if resp.status_code == 200:
257	                text = resp.text
258	                # Try to extract filer name from filing header
259	                if "COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:" in text:
260	                    start = text.index("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:") + len("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:")
261	                    end = text.index("\n", start) if "\n" in text[start:start+200] else start + 100

302	                    continue
303

305	                accession = accessions[i] if i < len(accessions) else ""
306	                primary_doc = primary_docs[i] if i < len(primary_docs) else ""
307	
308	                # Parse the Form 4 XML for transaction details

333	    def _parse_form4_xml(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
334	        """Parse a Form 4 XML document for insider transaction details."""
335	        transactions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
336	
337	        if not accession or not primary_doc:
338	            return transactions
339	
340	        try:
341	            acc_no_dashes = accession.replace("-", "")
342	            doc_url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{acc_no_dashes}/{primary_doc}"
343	            resp = self._get(doc_url)
344	
345	            if resp.status_code != 200:
346	                return transactions
347	
348	            # Form 4 filings are XML
349	            if not primary_doc.endswith(".xml"):
350	                return transactions
351	
352	            root = ElementTree.fromstring(resp.content)
353	
354	            # Extract reporting owner info
355	            owner_el = root.find(".//reportingOwner")
356	            insider_name = ""
357	            title = ""
358	            if owner_el is not None:
359	                owner_id = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerId")
360	                if owner_id is not None:
361	                    name_el = owner_id.find("rptOwnerName")
362	                    insider_name = name_el.text.strip() if name_el is not None and name_el.text else ""
363	
364	                relationship = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerRelationship")
365	                if relationship is not None:
366	                    title_el = relationship.find("officerTitle")
367	                    if title_el is not None and title_el.text:
368	                        title = title_el.text.strip()
369	                    elif relationship.find("isDirector") is not None:
370	                        is_dir = relationship.find("isDirector")
371	                        if is_dir is not None and is_dir.text and is_dir.text.strip() in ("1", "true"):
372	                            title = "Director"
373	
374	            # Extract non-derivative transactions
375	            for txn_el in root.findall(".//nonDerivativeTransaction"):
376	                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
377	                if txn:
378	                    transactions.append(txn)
379	
380	            # Also check derivative transactions
381	            for txn_el in root.findall(".//derivativeTransaction"):
382	                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
383	                if txn:
384	                    txn["transaction_type"] = f"Derivative {txn.get('transaction_type', '')}"
385	                    transactions.append(txn)
386	
387	        except (ElementTree.ParseError, requests.exceptions.RequestException) as e:
388	            logger.warning("edgar form4 XML parse: error for %s/%s: %s", cik, accession, e)
389	
390	        return transactions
391	
392	    def _extract_transaction(self, txn_el: ElementTree.Element, insider_name: str, title: str) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
393	        """Extract transaction details from a Form 4 XML transaction element."""
394	        coding = txn_el.find(".//transactionCoding")
395	        if coding is None:
396	            return None
397	
398	        code_el = coding.find("transactionCode")
399	        tx_code = code_el.text.strip() if code_el is not None and code_el.text else ""
400	
401	        # Map transaction codes to readable types
402	        code_map = {
403	            "P": "Purchase",
404	            "S": "Sale",
405	            "A": "Grant/Award",
406	            "D": "Disposition (non-open market)",
407	            "F": "Tax withholding",
408	            "M": "Option exercise",
409	            "G": "Gift",
410	            "C": "Conversion",
411	            "X": "Option expiration",
412	            "J": "Other",
413	        }
414	        transaction_type = code_map.get(tx_code, tx_code)
415	
416	        # Extract amounts
417	        amounts = txn_el.find(".//transactionAmounts")
418	        shares: Optional[float] = None
419	        price_per_share: Optional[float] = None
420	
421	        if amounts is not None:
422	            shares_el = amounts.find("transactionShares/value")
423	            if shares_el is not None and shares_el.text:
424	                try:
425	                    shares = float(shares_el.text.strip())
426	                except ValueError:
427	                    pass
428	
429	            price_el = amounts.find("transactionPricePerShare/value")
430	            if price_el is not None and price_el.text:
431	                try:
432	                    price_per_share = float(price_el.text.strip())
433	                except ValueError:
434	                    pass
435	
436	        total_value: Optional[float] = None
437	        if shares is not None and price_per_share is not None:
438	            total_value = round(shares * price_per_share, 2)
439	
440	        # Shares held after
441	        post_el = txn_el.find(".//postTransactionAmounts/sharesOwnedFollowingTransaction/value")
442	        shares_held_after: Optional[float] = None
443	        if post_el is not None and post_el.text:
444	            try:
445	                shares_held_after = float(post_el.text.strip())
446	            except ValueError:
447	                pass
448	
449	        return {
450	            "filing_date": "",  # Set by caller
451	            "insider_name": insider_name,
452	            "title": title,
453	            "transaction_type": transaction_type,
454	            "shares": shares,
455	            "price_per_share": price_per_share,
456	            "total_value": total_value,
457	            "shares_held_after": shares_held_after,
458	        }

"""SEC EDGAR client — institutional holdings (13F), position changes (13D/13G), insider trades (Form 4).

Provides EdgarClient with three main methods:
- get_institutional_holders(symbol): top institutional holders from 13F filings
- get_major_position_changes(symbol): recent 13D/13G filings
- get_insider_transactions(symbol): recent Form 4 insider transactions

User-Agent set per SEC EDGAR policy. Rate limit: 10 req/sec via throttle.
No API key required — SEC EDGAR is free public data.
"""

import logging
import time
from typing import Any, Optional
from xml.etree import ElementTree

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger("signal.edgar")

EDGAR_BASE_URL = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST"
EDGAR_DATA_URL = "https://data.sec.gov"
EDGAR_USER_AGENT = "InstantNews dev@instnews.net"

# Cache TTLs per acceptance criteria
INSTITUTIONAL_TTL = 86400  # 24 hours for 13F
POSITION_CHANGE_TTL = 21600  # 6 hours for 13D/13G
INSIDER_TTL = 3600  # 1 hour for Form 4

# SEC rate limit: 10 req/sec — minimum 0.1s between requests
_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL = 0.1


class _CacheEntry:
    __slots__ = ("value", "expires_at")

    def __init__(self, value: Any, ttl: float) -> None:
        self.value = value
        self.expires_at = time.monotonic() + ttl

    def is_valid(self) -> bool:
        return time.monotonic() < self.expires_at


class EdgarClient:
    """Client for SEC EDGAR filings with in-memory caching and rate limiting."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._session = requests.Session()
        self._session.headers.update({
            "User-Agent": EDGAR_USER_AGENT,
            "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
        })
        self._last_request_time = 0.0
        self._cik_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._institutional_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._position_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._insider_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}

    @property
    def enabled(self) -> bool:
        return True  # EDGAR is free, always enabled

    def _throttle(self) -> None:
        """Enforce SEC EDGAR 10 req/sec rate limit."""
        now = time.monotonic()
        elapsed = now - self._last_request_time
        if elapsed < _MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL:
            time.sleep(_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL - elapsed)
        self._last_request_time = time.monotonic()

    def _get(self, url: str, params: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
             timeout: int = 15) -> requests.Response:
        """Rate-limited GET request."""
        self._throttle()
        return self._session.get(url, params=params, timeout=timeout)

    def _resolve_cik(self, symbol: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Resolve ticker symbol to SEC CIK number (zero-padded to 10 digits)."""
        cached = self._cik_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        # Primary: use company tickers JSON for ticker → CIK mapping
        try:
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/files/company_tickers.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            tickers = resp.json()

            for entry in tickers.values():
                if entry.get("ticker", "").upper() == symbol:
                    cik_padded = str(entry["cik_str"]).zfill(10)
                    self._cik_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(cik_padded, 86400)
                    return cik_padded

        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar: CIK lookup failed for %s: %s", symbol, e)

        return None

    def get_institutional_holders(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 20) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch top institutional holders from latest 13F filings.

        Returns list of holders, each with: institution_name, shares_held, value,
        report_date, change_type.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._institutional_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            # Use EFTS full-text search to find 13F-HR filings mentioning this ticker
            resp = self._get(
                f"{EDGAR_BASE_URL}/search-index",
                params={
                    "q": f'"{symbol}"',
                    "forms": "13F-HR,13F-HR/A",
                    "from": "0",
                    "size": str(min(limit, 40)),
                },
            )

            holders: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            if resp.status_code == 200:
                data = resp.json()
                hits = data.get("hits", {}).get("hits", [])

                seen_filers: set[str] = set()
                for hit in hits:
                    source = hit.get("_source", {})
                    display_names = source.get("display_names", [])
                    filer_name = source.get("entity_name", display_names[0] if display_names else "Unknown")
                    filing_date = source.get("file_date", "")

                    if filer_name in seen_filers:
                        continue
                    seen_filers.add(filer_name)

                    holders.append({
                        "institution_name": filer_name,
                        "shares_held": None,
                        "value": None,
                        "report_date": filing_date,
                        "change_type": "held",
                    })

                    if len(holders) >= limit:
                        break

            self._institutional_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(holders, INSTITUTIONAL_TTL)
            return holders

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar 13F: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar 13F: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar 13F: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def get_major_position_changes(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 10) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch recent 13D/13G filings (major position changes) for a company.

        Returns list of filings, each with: filer_name, filing_date, filing_type,
        percent_owned, shares_held, change_description.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._position_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            cik = self._resolve_cik(symbol)
            if not cik:
                result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
                self._position_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(result, POSITION_CHANGE_TTL)
                return result

            # Fetch company submissions to find SC 13D and SC 13G filings
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/submissions/CIK{cik}.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data = resp.json()

            recent = data.get("filings", {}).get("recent", {})
            forms = recent.get("form", [])
            dates = recent.get("filingDate", [])
            accessions = recent.get("accessionNumber", [])
            primary_docs = recent.get("primaryDocument", [])

            results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            for i, form_type in enumerate(forms):
                if form_type not in ("SC 13D", "SC 13D/A", "SC 13G", "SC 13G/A"):
                    continue

                filing_date = dates[i] if i < len(dates) else ""
                accession = accessions[i] if i < len(accessions) else ""
                primary_doc = primary_docs[i] if i < len(primary_docs) else ""

                # Try to extract ownership details from the filing
                percent_owned = None
                shares_held_val = None
                filer_name = "Unknown Filer"

                # Attempt to fetch filing details
                if accession and primary_doc:
                    filer_name, percent_owned, shares_held_val = self._parse_13dg_filing(
                        cik, accession, primary_doc
                    )

                is_amendment = "/A" in form_type
                change_desc = f"{'Amended ' if is_amendment else ''}{'Schedule 13D' if '13D' in form_type else 'Schedule 13G'} filing"

                results.append({
                    "filer_name": filer_name,
                    "filing_date": filing_date,
                    "filing_type": form_type,
                    "percent_owned": percent_owned,
                    "shares_held": shares_held_val,
                    "change_description": change_desc,
                })

                if len(results) >= limit:
                    break

            self._position_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(results, POSITION_CHANGE_TTL)
            return results

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def _parse_13dg_filing(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> tuple[str, Optional[float], Optional[int]]:
        """Try to extract filer name and ownership from a 13D/13G filing."""
        filer_name = "Unknown Filer"
        percent_owned: Optional[float] = None
        shares_held: Optional[int] = None

        try:
            # Fetch the filing index to get filer info
            acc_no_dashes = accession.replace("-", "")
            index_url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{acc_no_dashes}/{accession}-index.htm"
            resp = self._get(index_url)
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                text = resp.text
                # Try to extract filer name from filing header
                if "COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:" in text:
                    start = text.index("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:") + len("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:")
                    end = text.index("\n", start) if "\n" in text[start:start+200] else start + 100
                    filer_name = text[start:end].strip()[:100]
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError):
            pass

        return filer_name, percent_owned, shares_held

    def get_insider_transactions(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 20) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch recent Form 4 insider transactions for a company.

        Returns list of transactions, each with: filing_date, insider_name, title,
        transaction_type, shares, price_per_share, total_value, shares_held_after.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._insider_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            cik = self._resolve_cik(symbol)
            if not cik:
                result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
                self._insider_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(result, INSIDER_TTL)
                return result

            # Fetch company submissions to find Form 4 filings
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/submissions/CIK{cik}.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data = resp.json()

            recent = data.get("filings", {}).get("recent", {})
            forms = recent.get("form", [])
            dates = recent.get("filingDate", [])
            accessions = recent.get("accessionNumber", [])
            primary_docs = recent.get("primaryDocument", [])

            results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            for i, form_type in enumerate(forms):
                if form_type not in ("4", "4/A"):
                    continue

                filing_date = dates[i] if i < len(dates) else ""
                accession = accessions[i] if i < len(accessions) else ""
                primary_doc = primary_docs[i] if i < len(primary_docs) else ""

                # Parse the Form 4 XML for transaction details
                transactions = self._parse_form4_xml(cik, accession, primary_doc)
                for txn in transactions:
                    txn["filing_date"] = filing_date
                    results.append(txn)

                    if len(results) >= limit:
                        break

                if len(results) >= limit:
                    break

            self._insider_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(results, INSIDER_TTL)
            return results

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar form4: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar form4: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar form4: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def _parse_form4_xml(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Parse a Form 4 XML document for insider transaction details."""
        transactions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

        if not accession or not primary_doc:
            return transactions

        try:
            acc_no_dashes = accession.replace("-", "")
            doc_url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{acc_no_dashes}/{primary_doc}"
            resp = self._get(doc_url)

            if resp.status_code != 200:
                return transactions

            # Form 4 filings are XML
            if not primary_doc.endswith(".xml"):
                return transactions

            root = ElementTree.fromstring(resp.content)

            # Extract reporting owner info
            owner_el = root.find(".//reportingOwner")
            insider_name = ""
            title = ""
            if owner_el is not None:
                owner_id = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerId")
                if owner_id is not None:
                    name_el = owner_id.find("rptOwnerName")
                    insider_name = name_el.text.strip() if name_el is not None and name_el.text else ""

                relationship = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerRelationship")
                if relationship is not None:
                    title_el = relationship.find("officerTitle")
                    if title_el is not None and title_el.text:
                        title = title_el.text.strip()
                    elif relationship.find("isDirector") is not None:
                        is_dir = relationship.find("isDirector")
                        if is_dir is not None and is_dir.text and is_dir.text.strip() in ("1", "true"):
                            title = "Director"

            # Extract non-derivative transactions
            for txn_el in root.findall(".//nonDerivativeTransaction"):
                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
                if txn:
                    transactions.append(txn)

            # Also check derivative transactions
            for txn_el in root.findall(".//derivativeTransaction"):
                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
                if txn:
                    txn["transaction_type"] = f"Derivative {txn.get('transaction_type', '')}"
                    transactions.append(txn)

        except (ElementTree.ParseError, requests.exceptions.RequestException) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar form4 XML parse: error for %s/%s: %s", cik, accession, e)

        return transactions

    def _extract_transaction(self, txn_el: ElementTree.Element, insider_name: str, title: str) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Extract transaction details from a Form 4 XML transaction element."""
        coding = txn_el.find(".//transactionCoding")
        if coding is None:
            return None

        code_el = coding.find("transactionCode")
        tx_code = code_el.text.strip() if code_el is not None and code_el.text else ""

        # Map transaction codes to readable types
        code_map = {
            "P": "Purchase",
            "S": "Sale",
            "A": "Grant/Award",
            "D": "Disposition (non-open market)",
            "F": "Tax withholding",
            "M": "Option exercise",
            "G": "Gift",
            "C": "Conversion",
            "X": "Option expiration",
            "J": "Other",
        }
        transaction_type = code_map.get(tx_code, tx_code)

        # Extract amounts
        amounts = txn_el.find(".//transactionAmounts")
        shares: Optional[float] = None
        price_per_share: Optional[float] = None

        if amounts is not None:
            shares_el = amounts.find("transactionShares/value")
            if shares_el is not None and shares_el.text:
                try:
                    shares = float(shares_el.text.strip())
                except ValueError:
                    pass

            price_el = amounts.find("transactionPricePerShare/value")
            if price_el is not None and price_el.text:
                try:
                    price_per_share = float(price_el.text.strip())
                except ValueError:
                    pass

        total_value: Optional[float] = None
        if shares is not None and price_per_share is not None:
            total_value = round(shares * price_per_share, 2)

        # Shares held after
        post_el = txn_el.find(".//postTransactionAmounts/sharesOwnedFollowingTransaction/value")
        shares_held_after: Optional[float] = None
        if post_el is not None and post_el.text:
            try:
                shares_held_after = float(post_el.text.strip())
            except ValueError:
                pass

        return {
            "filing_date": "",  # Set by caller
            "insider_name": insider_name,
            "title": title,
            "transaction_type": transaction_type,
            "shares": shares,
            "price_per_share": price_per_share,
            "total_value": total_value,
            "shares_held_after": shares_held_after,
        }

"""Tests for SEC EDGAR client — 13F, 13D/13G, Form 4 parsers."""

from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

from app.services.edgar_client import EdgarClient


# Sample Form 4 XML for testing
SAMPLE_FORM4_XML = b"""<?xml version="1.0"?>
<ownershipDocument>
  <reportingOwner>
    <reportingOwnerId>
      <rptOwnerName>DOE JOHN</rptOwnerName>
    </reportingOwnerId>
    <reportingOwnerRelationship>
      <officerTitle>CEO</officerTitle>
    </reportingOwnerRelationship>
  </reportingOwner>
  <nonDerivativeTable>
    <nonDerivativeTransaction>
      <transactionCoding>
        <transactionCode>P</transactionCode>
      </transactionCoding>
      <transactionAmounts>
        <transactionShares>
          <value>1000</value>
        </transactionShares>
        <transactionPricePerShare>
          <value>150.50</value>
        </transactionPricePerShare>
      </transactionAmounts>
      <postTransactionAmounts>
        <sharesOwnedFollowingTransaction>
          <value>5000</value>
        </sharesOwnedFollowingTransaction>
      </postTransactionAmounts>
    </nonDerivativeTransaction>
  </nonDerivativeTable>
</ownershipDocument>"""


class TestEdgarClientBasic:
    """Basic EdgarClient tests."""

    def test_always_enabled(self):
        client = EdgarClient()
        assert client.enabled is True

    def test_import_works(self):
        from app.services.edgar_client import EdgarClient
        assert EdgarClient is not None


class TestEdgarClientCIKLookup:
    """Tests for CIK resolution."""

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_resolve_cik_success(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }
        mock_get.return_value = mock_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        cik = client._resolve_cik("AAPL")
        assert cik == "0000320193"

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_resolve_cik_not_found(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }
        mock_get.return_value = mock_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        cik = client._resolve_cik("ZZZZZ")
        assert cik is None

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_resolve_cik_cached(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }
        mock_get.return_value = mock_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        client._resolve_cik("AAPL")
        client._resolve_cik("AAPL")
        # Only one request for the CIK lookup (cached)
        assert mock_get.call_count == 1


class TestEdgarClientForm4:
    """Tests for Form 4 parsing."""

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_insider_transactions_success(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        # CIK lookup response
        mock_cik_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_cik_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }

        # Submissions response with Form 4 filings
        mock_submissions_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_submissions_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.json.return_value = {
            "filings": {
                "recent": {
                    "form": ["4", "10-Q", "4"],
                    "filingDate": ["2025-01-15", "2025-01-10", "2025-01-05"],
                    "accessionNumber": ["0001-23-456789", "0002-23-456789", "0003-23-456789"],
                    "primaryDocument": ["form4.xml", "10q.htm", "form4.xml"],
                }
            }
        }

        # Form 4 XML response
        mock_xml_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_xml_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_xml_resp.content = SAMPLE_FORM4_XML

        mock_get.side_effect = [mock_cik_resp, mock_submissions_resp, mock_xml_resp, mock_xml_resp]

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_insider_transactions("AAPL")

        assert result is not None
        assert isinstance(result, list)
        assert len(result) > 0

        txn = result[0]
        assert txn["insider_name"] == "DOE JOHN"
        assert txn["title"] == "CEO"
        assert txn["transaction_type"] == "Purchase"
        assert txn["shares"] == 1000.0
        assert txn["price_per_share"] == 150.50
        assert txn["total_value"] == 150500.0
        assert txn["shares_held_after"] == 5000.0

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_insider_transactions_caching(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_cik_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_cik_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }

        mock_submissions_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_submissions_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.json.return_value = {
            "filings": {"recent": {"form": [], "filingDate": [], "accessionNumber": [], "primaryDocument": []}}
        }

        mock_get.side_effect = [mock_cik_resp, mock_submissions_resp]

        client = EdgarClient()
        result1 = client.get_insider_transactions("AAPL")
        result2 = client.get_insider_transactions("AAPL")

        assert result1 == result2
        # Second call should hit cache, so only 2 HTTP requests total
        assert mock_get.call_count == 2

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_insider_transactions_no_cik(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        # CIK lookup returns empty — ticker not in company_tickers.json
        mock_tickers = MagicMock()
        mock_tickers.status_code = 200
        mock_tickers.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_tickers.json.return_value = {}

        mock_get.return_value = mock_tickers

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_insider_transactions("INVALID")
        assert result is not None
        assert result == []


class TestEdgarClient13DG:
    """Tests for 13D/13G position changes."""

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_major_position_changes_success(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_cik_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_cik_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }

        mock_submissions_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_submissions_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.json.return_value = {
            "filings": {
                "recent": {
                    "form": ["SC 13G", "10-K", "SC 13D/A"],
                    "filingDate": ["2025-02-14", "2025-01-28", "2025-01-10"],
                    "accessionNumber": ["0001-25-000001", "0002-25-000002", "0003-25-000003"],
                    "primaryDocument": ["sc13g.htm", "10k.htm", "sc13da.htm"],
                }
            }
        }

        # Filing index response for 13D/G
        mock_index_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_index_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_index_resp.text = "COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:			VANGUARD GROUP\nSomething else"

        mock_get.side_effect = [mock_cik_resp, mock_submissions_resp, mock_index_resp, mock_index_resp]

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_major_position_changes("AAPL")

        assert result is not None
        assert isinstance(result, list)
        assert len(result) == 2  # SC 13G and SC 13D/A, not 10-K

        assert result[0]["filing_type"] == "SC 13G"
        assert result[0]["filing_date"] == "2025-02-14"
        assert "13G" in result[0]["change_description"]

        assert result[1]["filing_type"] == "SC 13D/A"
        assert "Amended" in result[1]["change_description"]

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_major_position_changes_empty(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_cik_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_cik_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_cik_resp.json.return_value = {
            "0": {"cik_str": 320193, "ticker": "AAPL", "title": "Apple Inc."}
        }

        mock_submissions_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_submissions_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        mock_submissions_resp.json.return_value = {
            "filings": {"recent": {"form": ["10-K", "10-Q"], "filingDate": ["2025-01-28", "2025-01-10"], "accessionNumber": ["a", "b"], "primaryDocument": ["a.htm", "b.htm"]}}
        }

        mock_get.side_effect = [mock_cik_resp, mock_submissions_resp]

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_major_position_changes("AAPL")
        assert result == []


class TestEdgarClient13F:
    """Tests for 13F institutional holders."""

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_institutional_holders_returns_list(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        # EFTS search response — single request
        mock_search_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_search_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_search_resp.json.return_value = {
            "hits": {
                "hits": [
                    {"_source": {"entity_name": "VANGUARD GROUP", "file_date": "2025-02-14"}},
                    {"_source": {"entity_name": "BLACKROCK INC", "file_date": "2025-02-10"}},
                ]
            }
        }

        mock_get.return_value = mock_search_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_institutional_holders("AAPL")

        assert result is not None
        assert isinstance(result, list)
        assert len(result) == 2
        assert result[0]["institution_name"] == "VANGUARD GROUP"
        assert result[1]["institution_name"] == "BLACKROCK INC"
        assert result[0]["report_date"] == "2025-02-14"

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_institutional_holders_deduplicates(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_search_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_search_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_search_resp.json.return_value = {
            "hits": {
                "hits": [
                    {"_source": {"entity_name": "VANGUARD GROUP", "file_date": "2025-02-14"}},
                    {"_source": {"entity_name": "VANGUARD GROUP", "file_date": "2025-01-14"}},
                    {"_source": {"entity_name": "BLACKROCK INC", "file_date": "2025-02-10"}},
                ]
            }
        }

        mock_get.return_value = mock_search_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        result = client.get_institutional_holders("AAPL")

        assert result is not None
        assert len(result) == 2  # Duplicate VANGUARD GROUP removed

    @patch("app.services.edgar_client.requests.Session.get")
    def test_institutional_holders_caching(self, mock_get: MagicMock):
        mock_search_resp = MagicMock()
        mock_search_resp.status_code = 200
        mock_search_resp.json.return_value = {"hits": {"hits": []}}

        mock_get.return_value = mock_search_resp

        client = EdgarClient()
        client.get_institutional_holders("AAPL")
        call_count_after_first = mock_get.call_count
        client.get_institutional_holders("AAPL")  # should hit cache
        assert mock_get.call_count == call_count_after_first

import requests, json

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InstantNews dev@instnews.net'}

# Test fetching the 13F info table from the first search result
resp = requests.get(
    'https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index',
    params={'q': '\"AAPL\"', 'forms': '13F-HR', 'from': '0', 'size': '5'},
    headers=headers, timeout=15
)
data = resp.json()
hits = data.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])

# Find a hit that has file_type INFORMATION TABLE
for hit in hits:
    src = hit.get('_source', {})
    ft = src.get('file_type', '')
    print(f'file_type={ft}, ciks={src.get(\"ciks\")}, adsh={src.get(\"adsh\")}, _id={hit[\"_id\"]}, period={src.get(\"period_ending\")}, name={src.get(\"display_names\", [\"?\"])[0][:50]}')
    
    if ft == 'INFORMATION TABLE':
        cik = src['ciks'][0].lstrip('0')
        adsh = src['adsh']
        adsh_nodash = adsh.replace('-', '')
        _id = hit['_id']
        filename = _id.split(':', 1)[1] if ':' in _id else None
        if filename:
            url = f'https://data.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{adsh_nodash}/{filename}'
            print(f'\\nFetching info table from: {url}')
            import time
            time.sleep(0.2)
            r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15)
            print(f'Status: {r.status_code}')
            if r.status_code == 200:
                # Show first 2000 chars
                text = r.text[:2000]
                print('Content preview:')
                print(text)
            break
"

import requests, time

filename = 'loip13f.xml'

urls = [
    f'https://data.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{adsh_nodash}/{filename}',

]

for url in urls:

    idx = r2.json()
    print(json.dumps(idx, indent=2)[:2000])

python3 -c "
import requests, json, time

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InstantNews dev@instnews.net'}

# First get the company name for AAPL from company_tickers.json
r = requests.get('https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json', headers=headers, timeout=15)
tickers = r.json()
aapl_title = None
for entry in tickers.values():
    if entry.get('ticker') == 'AAPL':
        aapl_title = entry['title']
        break
print(f'AAPL title from SEC: {aapl_title}')

time.sleep(0.2)

# Search for 13F info tables that mention 'APPLE INC' (the company name as it appears in 13F)
resp = requests.get(
    'https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index',
    params={'q': '\"APPLE INC\"', 'forms': '13F-HR', 'from': '0', 'size': '5'},
    headers=headers, timeout=15
)
data = resp.json()
hits = data.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])
print(f'\\nHits for \"APPLE INC\" in 13F: {len(hits)}')
for h in hits[:3]:
    src = h.get('_source', {})
    print(f'  {src.get(\"display_names\", [\"?\"])[0][:60]} | {src.get(\"file_date\")} | {src.get(\"file_type\")} | period={src.get(\"period_ending\")}')
"

input.command:
python3 -c "
import requests, json, time

headers = {'User-Agent': 'InstantNews dev@instnews.net'}

# Try the /search endpoint (not search-index) which may support date sorting
resp = requests.get(
    'https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index',
    params={
        'q': '\"APPLE INC\"',
        'forms': '13F-HR',
        'dateRange': 'custom',
        'startdt': '2024-06-01',
        'enddt': '2026-12-31',
        'from': '0',
        'size': '5',
    },
    headers=headers, timeout=15
)
data = resp.json()
hits = data.get('hits', {}).get('hits', [])
print(f'Hits with date filter: {len(hits)}')
for h in hits[:5]:
    src = h.get('_source', {})
    print(f'  {src.get(\"display_names\", [\"?\"])[0][:60]} | {src.get(\"file_date\")} | period={src.get(\"period_ending\")}')

# Also try fetching and parsing one of these info tables
if hits:
    src = hits[0].get('_source', {})
    cik = src['ciks'][0].lstrip('0') or '0'
    adsh_nodash = src['adsh'].replace('-', '')
    _id = hits[0]['_id']
    filename = _id.split(':', 1)[1] if ':' in _id else None
    if filename:
        time.sleep(0.2)
        url = f'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{adsh_nodash}/{filename}'
        r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15)
        print(f'\\nInfo table fetch: {r.status_code}')
        if r.status_code == 200:
            # Search for APPLE in the XML
            xml = r.text
            idx = xml.upper().find('APPLE')
            if idx >= 0:
                # Show surrounding context
                start = max(0, idx - 200)
                end = min(len(xml), idx + 500)
                print(f'Found APPLE at position {idx}:')
                print(xml[start:end])
"

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""SEC EDGAR client — institutional holdings (13F), position changes (13D/13G), insider trades (Form 4).

Provides EdgarClient with three main methods:
- get_institutional_holders(symbol): top institutional holders from 13F filings
- get_major_position_changes(symbol): recent 13D/13G filings
- get_insider_transactions(symbol): recent Form 4 insider transactions

User-Agent set per SEC EDGAR policy. Rate limit: 10 req/sec via throttle.
No API key required — SEC EDGAR is free public data.
"""

import logging
import time
from typing import Any, Optional
from xml.etree import ElementTree

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger("signal.edgar")

EDGAR_BASE_URL = "https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST"
EDGAR_DATA_URL = "https://data.sec.gov"
EDGAR_USER_AGENT = "InstantNews dev@instnews.net"

# Cache TTLs per acceptance criteria
INSTITUTIONAL_TTL = 86400  # 24 hours for 13F
POSITION_CHANGE_TTL = 21600  # 6 hours for 13D/13G
INSIDER_TTL = 3600  # 1 hour for Form 4

# SEC rate limit: 10 req/sec — minimum 0.1s between requests
_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL = 0.1


class _CacheEntry:
    __slots__ = ("value", "expires_at")

    def __init__(self, value: Any, ttl: float) -> None:
        self.value = value
        self.expires_at = time.monotonic() + ttl

    def is_valid(self) -> bool:
        return time.monotonic() < self.expires_at


class EdgarClient:
    """Client for SEC EDGAR filings with in-memory caching and rate limiting."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._session = requests.Session()
        self._session.headers.update({
            "User-Agent": EDGAR_USER_AGENT,
            "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
        })
        self._last_request_time = 0.0
        self._cik_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._institutional_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._position_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}
        self._insider_cache: dict[str, _CacheEntry] = {}

    @property
    def enabled(self) -> bool:
        return True  # EDGAR is free, always enabled

    def _throttle(self) -> None:
        """Enforce SEC EDGAR 10 req/sec rate limit."""
        now = time.monotonic()
        elapsed = now - self._last_request_time
        if elapsed < _MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL:
            time.sleep(_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL - elapsed)
        self._last_request_time = time.monotonic()

    def _get(self, url: str, params: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
             timeout: int = 15) -> requests.Response:
        """Rate-limited GET request."""
        self._throttle()
        return self._session.get(url, params=params, timeout=timeout)

    def _resolve_cik(self, symbol: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Resolve ticker symbol to SEC CIK number (zero-padded to 10 digits)."""
        cached = self._cik_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        # Primary: use company tickers JSON for ticker → CIK mapping
        try:
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/files/company_tickers.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            tickers = resp.json()

            for entry in tickers.values():
                if entry.get("ticker", "").upper() == symbol:
                    cik_padded = str(entry["cik_str"]).zfill(10)
                    self._cik_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(cik_padded, 86400)
                    return cik_padded

        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar: CIK lookup failed for %s: %s", symbol, e)

        return None

    def get_institutional_holders(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 20) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch top institutional holders from latest 13F filings.

        Returns list of holders, each with: institution_name, shares_held, value,
        report_date, change_type.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._institutional_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            # Use EFTS full-text search to find 13F-HR filings mentioning this ticker
            resp = self._get(
                f"{EDGAR_BASE_URL}/search-index",
                params={
                    "q": f'"{symbol}"',
                    "forms": "13F-HR,13F-HR/A",
                    "from": "0",
                    "size": str(min(limit, 40)),
                },
            )

            holders: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            if resp.status_code == 200:
                data = resp.json()
                hits = data.get("hits", {}).get("hits", [])

                seen_filers: set[str] = set()
                for hit in hits:
                    source = hit.get("_source", {})
                    display_names = source.get("display_names", [])
                    filer_name = source.get("entity_name", display_names[0] if display_names else "Unknown")
                    filing_date = source.get("file_date", "")

                    if filer_name in seen_filers:
                        continue
                    seen_filers.add(filer_name)

                    holders.append({
                        "institution_name": filer_name,
                        "shares_held": None,
                        "value": None,
                        "report_date": filing_date,
                        "change_type": "held",
                    })

                    if len(holders) >= limit:
                        break

            self._institutional_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(holders, INSTITUTIONAL_TTL)
            return holders

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar 13F: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar 13F: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar 13F: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def get_major_position_changes(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 10) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch recent 13D/13G filings (major position changes) for a company.

        Returns list of filings, each with: filer_name, filing_date, filing_type,
        percent_owned, shares_held, change_description.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._position_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            cik = self._resolve_cik(symbol)
            if not cik:
                result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
                self._position_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(result, POSITION_CHANGE_TTL)
                return result

            # Fetch company submissions to find SC 13D and SC 13G filings
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/submissions/CIK{cik}.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data = resp.json()

            recent = data.get("filings", {}).get("recent", {})
            forms = recent.get("form", [])
            dates = recent.get("filingDate", [])
            accessions = recent.get("accessionNumber", [])
            primary_docs = recent.get("primaryDocument", [])

            results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            for i, form_type in enumerate(forms):
                if form_type not in ("SC 13D", "SC 13D/A", "SC 13G", "SC 13G/A"):
                    continue

                filing_date = dates[i] if i < len(dates) else ""
                accession = accessions[i] if i < len(accessions) else ""
                primary_doc = primary_docs[i] if i < len(primary_docs) else ""

                # Try to extract ownership details from the filing
                percent_owned = None
                shares_held_val = None
                filer_name = "Unknown Filer"

                # Attempt to fetch filing details
                if accession and primary_doc:
                    filer_name, percent_owned, shares_held_val = self._parse_13dg_filing(
                        cik, accession, primary_doc
                    )

                is_amendment = "/A" in form_type
                change_desc = f"{'Amended ' if is_amendment else ''}{'Schedule 13D' if '13D' in form_type else 'Schedule 13G'} filing"

                results.append({
                    "filer_name": filer_name,
                    "filing_date": filing_date,
                    "filing_type": form_type,
                    "percent_owned": percent_owned,
                    "shares_held": shares_held_val,
                    "change_description": change_desc,
                })

                if len(results) >= limit:
                    break

            self._position_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(results, POSITION_CHANGE_TTL)
            return results

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar 13D/G: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def _parse_13dg_filing(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> tuple[str, Optional[float], Optional[int]]:
        """Try to extract filer name and ownership from a 13D/13G filing."""
        filer_name = "Unknown Filer"
        percent_owned: Optional[float] = None
        shares_held: Optional[int] = None

        try:
            # Fetch the filing index to get filer info
            acc_no_dashes = accession.replace("-", "")
            index_url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{acc_no_dashes}/{accession}-index.htm"
            resp = self._get(index_url)
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                text = resp.text
                # Try to extract filer name from filing header
                if "COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:" in text:
                    start = text.index("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:") + len("COMPANY CONFORMED NAME:")
                    end = text.index("\n", start) if "\n" in text[start:start+200] else start + 100
                    filer_name = text[start:end].strip()[:100]
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError):
            pass

        return filer_name, percent_owned, shares_held

    def get_insider_transactions(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 20) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
        """Fetch recent Form 4 insider transactions for a company.

        Returns list of transactions, each with: filing_date, insider_name, title,
        transaction_type, shares, price_per_share, total_value, shares_held_after.
        """
        symbol = symbol.upper().strip()
        cached = self._insider_cache.get(symbol)
        if cached and cached.is_valid():
            return cached.value

        try:
            cik = self._resolve_cik(symbol)
            if not cik:
                result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
                self._insider_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(result, INSIDER_TTL)
                return result

            # Fetch company submissions to find Form 4 filings
            url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/submissions/CIK{cik}.json"
            resp = self._get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            data = resp.json()

            recent = data.get("filings", {}).get("recent", {})
            forms = recent.get("form", [])
            dates = recent.get("filingDate", [])
            accessions = recent.get("accessionNumber", [])
            primary_docs = recent.get("primaryDocument", [])

            results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            for i, form_type in enumerate(forms):
                if form_type not in ("4", "4/A"):
                    continue

                filing_date = dates[i] if i < len(dates) else ""
                accession = accessions[i] if i < len(accessions) else ""
                primary_doc = primary_docs[i] if i < len(primary_docs) else ""

                # Parse the Form 4 XML for transaction details
                transactions = self._parse_form4_xml(cik, accession, primary_doc)
                for txn in transactions:
                    txn["filing_date"] = filing_date
                    results.append(txn)

                    if len(results) >= limit:
                        break

                if len(results) >= limit:
                    break

            self._insider_cache[symbol] = _CacheEntry(results, INSIDER_TTL)
            return results

        except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
            if e.response is not None and e.response.status_code == 404:
                logger.warning("edgar form4: ticker %s not found", symbol)
            else:
                logger.warning("edgar form4: HTTP error for %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None
        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError, KeyError) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar form4: error fetching %s: %s", symbol, e)
            return None

    def _parse_form4_xml(self, cik: str, accession: str, primary_doc: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Parse a Form 4 XML document for insider transaction details."""
        transactions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

        if not accession or not primary_doc:
            return transactions

        try:
            acc_no_dashes = accession.replace("-", "")
            doc_url = f"{EDGAR_DATA_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik}/{acc_no_dashes}/{primary_doc}"
            resp = self._get(doc_url)

            if resp.status_code != 200:
                return transactions

            # Form 4 filings are XML
            if not primary_doc.endswith(".xml"):
                return transactions

            root = ElementTree.fromstring(resp.content)

            # Extract reporting owner info
            owner_el = root.find(".//reportingOwner")
            insider_name = ""
            title = ""
            if owner_el is not None:
                owner_id = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerId")
                if owner_id is not None:
                    name_el = owner_id.find("rptOwnerName")
                    insider_name = name_el.text.strip() if name_el is not None and name_el.text else ""

                relationship = owner_el.find("reportingOwnerRelationship")
                if relationship is not None:
                    title_el = relationship.find("officerTitle")
                    if title_el is not None and title_el.text:
                        title = title_el.text.strip()
                    elif relationship.find("isDirector") is not None:
                        is_dir = relationship.find("isDirector")
                        if is_dir is not None and is_dir.text and is_dir.text.strip() in ("1", "true"):
                            title = "Director"

            # Extract non-derivative transactions
            for txn_el in root.findall(".//nonDerivativeTransaction"):
                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
                if txn:
                    transactions.append(txn)

            # Also check derivative transactions
            for txn_el in root.findall(".//derivativeTransaction"):
                txn = self._extract_transaction(txn_el, insider_name, title)
                if txn:
                    txn["transaction_type"] = f"Derivative {txn.get('transaction_type', '')}"
                    transactions.append(txn)

        except (ElementTree.ParseError, requests.exceptions.RequestException) as e:
            logger.warning("edgar form4 XML parse: error for %s/%s: %s", cik, accession, e)

        return transactions

    def _extract_transaction(self, txn_el: ElementTree.Element, insider_name: str, title: str) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Extract transaction details from a Form 4 XML transaction element."""
        coding = txn_el.find(".//transactionCoding")
        if coding is None:
            return None

        code_el = coding.find("transactionCode")
        tx_code = code_el.text.strip() if code_el is not None and code_el.text else ""

        # Map transaction codes to readable types
        code_map = {
            "P": "Purchase",
            "S": "Sale",
            "A": "Grant/Award",
            "D": "Disposition (non-open market)",
            "F": "Tax withholding",
            "M": "Option exercise",
            "G": "Gift",
            "C": "Conversion",
            "X": "Option expiration",
            "J": "Other",
        }
        transaction_type = code_map.get(tx_code, tx_code)

        # Extract amounts
        amounts = txn_el.find(".//transactionAmounts")
        shares: Optional[float] = None
        price_per_share: Optional[float] = None

        if amounts is not None:
            shares_el = amounts.find("transactionShares/value")
            if shares_el is not None and shares_el.text:
                try:
                    shares = float(shares_el.text.strip())
                except ValueError:
                    pass

            price_el = amounts.find("transactionPricePerShare/value")
            if price_el is not None and price_el.text:
                try:
                    price_per_share = float(price_el.text.strip())
                except ValueError:
                    pass

        total_value: Optional[float] = None
        if shares is not None and price_per_share is not None:
            total_value = round(shares * price_per_share, 2)

        # Shares held after
        post_el = txn_el.find(".//postTransactionAmounts/sharesOwnedFollowingTransaction/value")
        shares_held_after: Optional[float] = None
        if post_el is not None and post_el.text:
            try:
                shares_held_after = float(post_el.text.strip())
            except ValueError:
                pass

        return {
            "filing_date": "",  # Set by caller
            "insider_name": insider_name,
            "title": title,
            "transaction_type": transaction_type,
            "shares": shares,
            "price_per_share": price_per_share,
            "total_value": total_value,
            "shares_held_after": shares_held_after,
        }
